findings.evidence: Jet tells the crew, "Somebody used a dead informant's access to stick a bounty on me that shouldn't exist," at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334.
findings.problem: This prematurely exposes Jet's dead-informant clue, contradicting the continuity sheet's knowledge-state that keeps that access-path fact hidden from the crew until chapter 11. The chapter can still give Jet a private reason to pursue without cashing the later reveal early.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the bridge confession at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:324 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334 so Jet names only the actionable pressure already safe to surface now: a dormant bounty, an old file, or a private mess that has become mobile again. Do not mention a dead informant, informant access, or any equivalent code-path explanation in this chapter.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:324-335 can tell Jet has his own urgent reason to chase the ship, but cannot yet infer that a dead informant's credentials were used on his file; fail if the revised passage still names an informant, dead access, or a synonymous reveal.

findings.evidence: The narration states "Faye hated beats like that" at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294 and "He absorbed that like a blow he knew he had earned" at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:300.
findings.problem: In a chapter otherwise held close to Spike, these sentences enter Faye's and Jet's inner states without a marked POV turn. That breaks the style-bible requirement for close third control and makes the scene's camera feel sloppy rather than intentionally ensemble.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:300, either rephrase through Spike's observations of face, posture, delay, and tone, or make a clearly sustained POV handoff before naming another character's private knowledge. Do not let single throwaway sentences slip inside another head.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294-300 reads either as clean Spike-observed inference or as an unmistakable new focalizer for at least a full paragraph before interior access; fail if the revised text still flatly tells the reader what Faye hates or what Jet knows without a clear POV shift.

findings.evidence: From snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390, the bridge sequence runs for well over ten consecutive dialogue/action paragraphs before Spike's next internal beat returns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:392.
findings.problem: That long exterior stretch drains the focalizer's interior pressure and makes the bridge section read like screenplay coverage. The cost is highest right before Spike lies to Jet: the chapter has moved his body through the scene, but not kept the reader close enough to the choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the bridge run from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348 to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390 to add Spike's internal experience at pressure points: when Jet preheats, when the launch becomes a fact, and immediately before Jet asks for honesty about the band. Use thought, sensation, or self-assessment, not just extra blocking.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348-390 contains at least three separate interior beats from Spike, including one after engine preheat starts and one before Jet's request at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390; fail if the scene can still be read as a long surface-only exchange with Spike's next real interiority deferred until after the promise.

findings.evidence: The bridge argument gives Faye a clean motive statement at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290, Spike one at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:302, Ed one at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:312, and Jet one at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334.
findings.problem: The scene turns each character's reason for pursuit into a tidy, self-explanatory speech beat. Instead of sounding overheard and pressured, the exchange starts sounding arranged so the chapter can lay out four motives in order. That is exactly the kind of balanced mini-speech polish the constitution warns against.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:302, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:312, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334, roughen at least two motive reveals so they arrive through interruption, partial admission, deflection, or a wrong first answer before the usable reason emerges. Keep the information, but stop letting everyone summarize themselves cleanly.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290-342, at least two characters' motives must be inferred from messy pressured speech rather than a complete self-summary, and no four-speaker run reads as four consecutive motive speeches; fail if the revised scene still presents the crew's reasons as neat one-by-one declarations.

findings.evidence: The chapter reuses the same negation-correction construction at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19 ("It wasn't because of memory"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51 ("It wasn't a ledger now"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71 ("It wasn't paranoia"), and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332 ("It wasn't louder. It was tighter").
findings.problem: Once that corrective sentence shape appears this many times in one chapter, it stops reading as chosen emphasis and starts reading as autopilot. The repeated rhythm flattens otherwise strong prose by teaching the reader exactly how the sentence will pivot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the local passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332 so at least two of them render the idea directly instead of through an "It wasn't X / it was Y" correction. Vary syntax and rhetorical approach rather than swapping synonyms into the same frame.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332, the negation-correction template appears no more than twice and the revised beats read with clearly different sentence movement on reread; fail if the same corrective pivot still dominates three or more of those spots.

summary: Strong Mars texture and a credible Spike locker sequence are not enough to pass this chapter. It breaks continuity by revealing Jet's dead-informant clue too early, then weakens the bridge movement with POV leakage, a long interiority drought, tidy motive speeches, and repeated corrective prose formulas.
